title: oxalis madrensis
intro: Oxalis madrensis, also known as the Mexican wood sorrel, is a type of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae). It has been traditionally used in Mexican folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The leaves and stems of the Oxalis madrensis plant contain anthraquinone glycosides and flavonoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Oxalis madrensis is often prepared as an infusion or decoction for its medicinal uses, and it can also be used as a natural food additive, adding a tangy flavor to salads and other dishes.
